entry.title: NY Times Books: 15 Jun, 2022
entry.summary: NY Times Books: 15 Jun, 2022 published at 2022-06-15 02:06AM.
entry.content:
Articles in this issue:
  • In ‘Also a Poet,’ a Search for Frank O’Hara and for Peace With Dad
  • James Patterson Apologizes for Saying White Writers Face a ‘Form of Racism’
  • A.B. Yehoshua, Israeli Writer, Dies at 85
  • Book Review: ‘The British Surrealists,’ by Desmond Morris
  • Book Review: “O Say Can You Hear? A Cultural Biography of ‘The Star-Spangled Banner,’” by Mark Clague
  • Book Review: “Esmond and Ilia,” by Marina Warner
  • Book Review: ‘Rough Draft,’ by Katy Tur
  • Review: “Elusive: How Peter Higgs Solved the Mystery of Mass,” by Frank Close
  • Review: “We Carry Their Bones,” Erin Kimmerle
  • Review: “Grand Hotel Europa,” by Ilja Leonard Pfeijffer
  • Review: “Diary of a Film,” by Niven Govinden
  • Book Review: “Moonlight and the Pearler’s Daughter” by Lizzie Pook
  • Review: “Jackie & Me,” by Louis Bayard
  • Book Review: “Pig Years” by Ellen Gaydos
  • Tom Perrotta on the Return of Tracy Flick
  • Peter Lamborn Wilson, Advocate of ‘Poetic Terrorism,’ Dies at 76
  • Ottessa Moshfegh’s ‘Lapvona’ Is Gloomy Folk Horror Set Long Ago
  • Swapping Books Brings Katy Tur Closer to Her Mother
  • John Merriman, Eminent Historian of France, Is Dead at 75
  • Review: ‘The Men,’ by Sandra Newman; and ‘When Women Were Dragons,’ by Kelly Barnhill
  • Abortion Politics, Money and the Reshaping of the G.O.P.
  • The Very Human Pleasure of Being Read To
  • “Red Scare” by Liam Francis Walsh
  • “The Golden Twine” (Cat’s Cradle, Volume 1) by Jo Rioux
  • Book Review: ‘The Twilight World,’ by Werner Herzog
The latest book reviews, best sellers, news and features from The NY TImes critics and reporters.

entry.title: Asian Review of Books: 14 Jun, 2022
entry.summary: Asian Review of Books: 14 Jun, 2022 published at 2022-06-14 23:00PM.
entry.content:
Articles in this issue:
  • “The History and Culture of Iran and Central Asia: From the Pre-Islamic to the Islamic Period”, edited by DG Tor and Minoru Inaba
  • “Fish Swimming in Dappled Sunlight” by Riku Onda
  • New Book Announcement: “A Flutter in the Colony” by Sandeep Ray
  • “Science on the Roof of the World: Empire and the remaking of the Himalaya” by Lachlan Fleetwood
  • “Death on Gokumon Island” by Seishi Yokomizo
  • “The Immortal King Rao” by Vauhini Vara
  • Podcast with Shiv Kunal Verma, author of “1965: A Western Sunrise”
  • New Book Announcement: “Self-Portrait With Ghost” by Meng Jin
  • “We Measure the Earth With Our Bodies” by Tsering Yangzom Lama
  • “Moro Warrior: A Philippine Chieftain, an American Schoolmaster, and The Untold Story of the Most Remarkable Resistance Fighters of World War II in the Pacific” by Thomas McKenna
In addition to reviewing books about or of relevance to Asia, the Asian Review of Books also features long-format essays by leading Asian writers and thinkers, to providing an unparalleled forum for discussion of key contemporary issues by Asians for Asia and a vehicle of intellectual depth and breadth where leading thinkers can write on the books, arts and ideas of the day. Widely quoted and referenced, with an archive of more than one thousand book reviews, it is the only web resource dedicated to Asian books. And now, with the addition of the new premium content, the Asian Review of Books, is a must-read publication.

entry.title: Five Books: 14 Jun, 2022
entry.content:
Articles in this issue:
  • David George Haskell on Natural History Books
  • Gary Gerstle on Neoliberalism Books
  • Helen Scales on The Best Ocean Novels for 10-14 Year Olds
  • Jan Loeys on Market Concentration Books
  • Rosa Rankin-Gee on The Best Near-Future Dystopias
  • Cal Flyn on The Notable Novels of Summer 2022
  • Benno Gammerl on Queer History Books
  • Matthew Brookes & Zack Raffin on Wanderlust Books
  • Sophie Roell on Notable Nonfiction of Spring 2022
  • Alison King on Best Verse Novels for 8-12 Year Olds
  • Sue Rainsford on Literary Horror Books
  • Howard Smith on Antitrust Books
  • Carole Hillenbrand on The Best History Books: the 2022 Wolfson Prize Shortlist
  • Velma Šarić on Bosnia Books
  • Mark Webber on NATO Books
  • Wajahat Ali on The Best South Asian American Novels
  • Chris Power on The Best Literary Thrillers
  • Michele Cobb on The Best Audiobooks: the 2022 Audie Awards
  • Serhii Plokhy on Ukraine and Russia Books
  • Sophie Roell on Notable Nonfiction of Early 2022
  • Cal Flyn on Notable Novels of Spring 2022
  • Nigel Warburton on The Best Philosophy Books of 2021
  • Jeffrey Archer on The Best Detective Fiction
  • Marion Turner on Best Medieval Historical Fiction
  • Tanya Selvaratnam on Domestic Violence Books
Expert book recommendations

entry.title: London Review of Books: 9 Jun, 2022
entry.summary: London Review of Books: 9 Jun, 2022 published at 2022-06-10 15:48PM.
entry.content:
Articles in this issue:
  • Madeleine Schwartz - ‘You got up and you died’
  • Letters
  • Miranda Carter - My Castaway This Week
  • Robin Bunce and Paul Field - Jubilee 1977
  • Jonathan Meades - The Palace Papers
  • William Davies - The Return of Inequality: Social Change and the Weight of the Past  / Colonialism and Modern Social Theory  / A Brief History of Equality
  • Frances Morgan - At Serpentine North: ‘Radio Ballads’
  • Colin Burrow - Here and There: Sites of Philosophy
  • Michael Ondaatje - Poem: ‘The Leg Glance’
  • Peter Howarth - How to Wash a Heart
  • Hugh Pennington - Monkeypox
  • Jo Applin - The Flowering: The Autobiography of Judy Chicago
  • Diarmaid MacCulloch - Zwingli: God’s Armed Prophet
  • Alexander Clapp - The Greek Revolution: 1821 and the Making of Modern Europe
  • Jen Stout - Short Cuts: In Kharkiv
  • Dennis Duncan - The Themerson Archive Catalogue
  • Sarah Resnick - The Candy House
  • Rosina Buckland - On Kyōsai
  • J. Robert Lennon - A Carnival of Snackery: Diaries 2003-20
  • Ben Crair - The Largest Avian Radiation: The Evolution of Perching Birds, or the Order Passeriformes
  • Tom Shippey - The Dragon in the West: From Ancient Myth to Modern Legend
  • Stephanie Burt - Diary: D&D
Literary review publishing essay-length book reviews and topical articles on politics, literature, history, philosophy, science and the arts by leading writers and thinkers
